summary: Fixed 2 follow-up findings from Greptile's round-3 review: round-2 made the cache keys scope-unique (category/property/compare-set) but the stored records still didn't carry those fields, and the read-side local-analysis commands (changes/opportunities/seasonality) had no way to avoid blending multiple scoped series together.
reason: Persisted category (gt_related_term) and category+property+compare_scope (gt_interest_point) as fields on the stored JSON records, not just folded into the opaque cache ID. Added filterInterestRowsToLatestScope/filterRelatedRowsToLatestScope helpers that narrow a keyword's queried rows to just the most-recently-synced scope before any diffing/averaging/ranking happens, applied in trends_changes.go, trends_opportunities.go, and trends_seasonality.go. This matches these commands' existing latest-sync-instant convention rather than introducing new --category/--property CLI flags on three more commands.
validated_outcome: Live dogfood full matrix: 65/65 pass. Manually confirmed live: syncing coffee under category 0 then category 71 leaves both scopes in the local store (53 rows each, verified via direct SQL), and trends seasonality subsequently reads only the latest-synced scope (category 71) instead of blending both.
